title: Holi-Spatial: Evolving Video Streams into Holistic 3D Spatial Intelligence
abstract: Spatial intelligence is fundamental to embodied AI, robotics, and AR/VR, relying heavily on large-scale 3D data. However, existing methods depend on manually annotated datasets rather than systematically annotating new large-scale 3D scenes from raw web data, resulting in scalability constraints and domain gaps. We propose Holi-Spatial, the first fully automated, large-scale, spatially-aware multimodal dataset, constructed from raw video inputs without human intervention. Holi-Spatial supports multi-level spatial supervision, including 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) reconstructions with depth maps, object-level and relational semantic annotations, and spatial QA pairs. We further build Holi-Spatial-4M, the first large-scale, high-quality 3D semantic dataset, containing 12K optimized 3DGS scenes, 1.3M 2D masks, 320K 3D bounding boxes, 320K instance captions, 1.2M 3D grounding instances, and 1.2M spatial QA pairs spanning geometric, relational, and semantic reasoning tasks. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our dataset outperforms existing methods on ScanNet, ScanNet++, and DL3DV benchmarks, and fine-tuning VLMs on Holi-Spatial-4M yields substantial improvements in model performance.
note: arXiv preprint submitted 2026-03-08. Listed as ICML 2026 by user but arXiv page shows no formal venue. cs.CV category.
